expectations.text: A preview table is shown before any task files are written, listing each task with its number, slug, title, and inferred dependencies
expectations.evidence: output.md lines 8-13 show a preview table with #, slug, title, deps columns and a 'Proceed?' prompt before task creation begins

expectations.text: The user is asked to confirm before task files are created
expectations.evidence: output.md line 15: 'Proceed? (confirm or adjust)' appears before the task creation section

expectations.text: Exactly 3 task specs are produced, one per plan step
expectations.evidence: task-1.md (Task 26), task-2.md (Task 27), task-3.md (Task 28) — confirmed in run summary

expectations.text: Each task spec contains all required create-task sections: Scope, Context, Goals, Implementation, Acceptance criteria, and Verification
expectations.evidence: task-1.md contains all sections including Current state, Desired state, Start here sub-sections, code in Implementation, binary checklist in Acceptance criteria, and bash blocks in Verification

expectations.text: Each task spec has a frontmatter block with ref, feature, priority, and status fields
expectations.evidence: task-1.md frontmatter: ref: general/26-add-api-rate-limiting, feature: general, priority: normal, status: todo

expectations.text: Step 2 (tests) is marked as depending on Step 1 (the feature) — not Independent — because tests require the feature to exist
expectations.evidence: Run summary confirms: 'Task 27 (Write tests): Depends on Task 26'. Preview table shows 'Depends on 26' for task 27.

expectations.text: Goals section contains 3 or more independently verifiable Must statements
expectations.evidence: task-1.md Goals section has 5 Must statements, each independently verifiable (sliding window algorithm, Redis storage, 429 + Retry-After, apply to all routes, no breakage)
